description: Easy to mold, compress, or yield to pressure; not hard or firm. 'Wuloe' names the quality of yielding—surfaces that give way, textures that comfort, resistance that accepts rather than fights.
sound_symbolism: The word yields gently. 'Wu' begins with rounded yielding, lips giving way. Then 'lo' flows low, pressure released. Finally 'e' opens and releases, the softness complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'wuloe shumu' (soft moss). Opposite of hard. Related to 'moli' (gentle) but emphasizes texture and physical quality rather than manner.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Softness in texture and form was beloved by Art Nouveau—the yielding curve, the gentle surface.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Soft speech, soft approach—the quality that de-escalates and welcomes.
